$comment: Per-entry readiness contract for the local catalog test harness. The harness reads `type_defaults` keyed by catalog_entry.type, then applies per-entry overrides on top. See scripts/catalog-tests/README.md for the kind vocabulary.

runtime.$comment: Runtimes ship no application — they're a base image waiting for client code. Asserting HTTP behavior would either 403 (no index) or 5xx; pod Ready is the meaningful signal.

static.$comment: Empty htdocs/document-root may serve a Welcome page (200), an autoindex (200), 403 Forbidden, or 404 Not Found. All four prove the static server is up. Strict /health probe is fragile across nginx/apache versions.

database.$comment: engine-specific ping via kubectl exec into the deployed DB pod

application.$comment: Apps almost-always 200 the landing page or 30x → /login; some serve 401/403 unauthenticated which is also a positive signal that the app is up. 5xx (or wget failing entirely) is the unambiguous failure.

rocketchat.$comment: DECISION NEEDED: rocketchat boots and connects to MongoDB (URL-encoded password works) but hits two issues that need bigger fixes: (1) needs MongoDB --replSet rs0 + rs.initiate() for change-streams (catalog mongo component runs standalone), (2) Node.js OOM at 500MB — needs 2-4Gi memory (manifest minimum=512Mi, but rocketchat:8 v6+ needs more). Verify on real staging with appropriate resources + replica-set mongo, OR add MongoDB replSet support to the catalog.

moodle-bitnami.$comment: DECISION NEEDED: bitnami/moodle retired late 2025; the catalog manifest still references bitnami:5.0 which 404s. Replacement options: (a) build a custom Dockerfile from moodlehq/moodle-php-apache:8.1 + moodle source download + config.php template, (b) drop the bitnami flavor and add a moodlehq-based variant, (c) skip from catalog. Skip until decided.

bookstack.$comment: Bookstack runs APP_KEY:generate + db:migrate at first boot — slow on starter plan

n8n.$comment: n8n redirects / to /setup on first run

discourse.$comment: DECISION NEEDED: bitnami/discourse retired late 2025; discourse/discourse:latest is the discourse_docker base meant for the launcher tool, doesn't run standalone. Building a runnable Dockerfile from discourse_docker source is 2-4h of work. Alternatives: (a) build catalog-side Dockerfile, (b) drop from catalog. Skip until decided.

strapi.$comment: DECISION NEEDED: naskio/strapi:5.30.1 fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND on staticFiles.js — yarn-global install is broken in the image. Alternatives: (a) build a catalog-side Dockerfile from node:22-alpine + npx create-strapi-app + healthcheck, (b) use strapi/strapi:latest official (scaffold pattern), (c) drop from catalog. Skip until decided.

invoiceninja.$comment: DECISION NEEDED: invoiceninja-debian:5 is FPM-only (port 9000) — no nginx bundled. App boots correctly (db seed, queue workers, scheduler all up) but doesn't serve HTTP on port 80. Needs an nginx sidecar component proxying to FPM:9000 (upstream's docker-compose example), OR a different image. Manifest's mount paths + IN_USER_EMAIL/IN_PASSWORD env are correct as-is and will be reused when nginx is added.

coturn.$comment: DinD docker-compose.local.yml does not publish hostPort 3478. The pod boots fine in-cluster but external STUN probe is unreachable. Verify in real staging via integration-staging.sh, not local DinD.

jitsi.$comment: jitsi has 5 components; `web` is the ingress target. Without this override the probe falls through to coturn (skipped via DB_PORTS) or jicofo.
